title: pteridium esculentum subsp. gryphus
intro: Pteridium esculentum subsp. gryphus, commonly known as bracken, is a subspecies of fern native to the Northern Hemisphere, particularly found in temperate and subarctic regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor activities. Bracken has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially inhibiting the growth of certain cancer cells. It can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and the need for further research on its safety and efficacy.
